title: No Ender Pearl Damage
body:
![KawaMood's No Ender Pearl Damage](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/fcaa0c6dd2a3266ff1dd81465c35d6d4790b8e94.jpeg)

# **No Ender Pearl Damage**

**Travel without being hurt!**

# 🪄 Introduction

**No Ender Pearl Damage** is a data pack that removes the damage players take when they are teleported to an ender pearl they have thrown.

Damage you get from ender pearls can be frustrating, as it forces player to consume food and wait for their health to regenerate before using another ender pearl. It also damages armors.
It can be annoying especially when you're trying to summon an endermite for any kind of purposes.

# ⚙️Settings

## Do Endermite Spawning

By default with this data pack, Endermites spawn from thrown ender pearls with 5% chance if the owner wasn't in creative mode when they threw it and if the gamerule `doMobSpawning` is set to `true`.

However, you can completely **disable** Endermite spawning, **enable** it back, or **get** the current state of the setting with the following commands respectively:
```
/function pk_no_en:settings/do_endermite_spawning/false
/function pk_no_en:settings/do_endermite_spawning/true
/function pk_no_en:settings/do_endermite_spawning/get
```

# 🧹 Uninstall

No Ender Pearl comes with an uninstallation process. Running it will:
- Remove all scores and storage specific to the current data pack
- If there is no more KawaMood data packs installed, also remove all common scores and storages

The command to start the uninstalling process is the following one: 
```
/function pk_no_en:cmd/uninstall
```

# 🪠 Report an issue

If you encountered an issue with this data pack, preferably join the [Discord server](https://discord.com/invite/w8s9XWgN6v), and open a post in the dedicated **#data-pack-issues** channel to describe your problem. Please, don't forget to provide all necessary informations including:
- The Minecraft version you're using.
- The version of the concerned data pack you're using.
- If you're using any modified version like Forge, Paper or Spigot.
- If you do, the plugins or mods you're using that may alter the functioning of data packs.
- The list of other data packs you're using, if you have others.
- A clear description of your issue, and a way to reproduce it.
- If necessary, a video or a screenshot of the issue in game.

# 📌 Other links
